name: yashimajinumi-no-kami
shortDescription: Yashimajinumi-no-Kami is a Shinto deity associated with storms and sea, considered one of the offspring of Susanoo.
description: Yashimajinumi-no-Kami is a Shinto deity prominent in Japanese mythology, particularly associated with storms, the sea, and potentially wind and thunder, aligning with the broader sphere of influence of his father, Susanoo. He is considered one of the children born from Susanoo when he plucked out his beard hairs and scattered them. While detailed individual myths specifically centered on Yashimajinumi-no-Kami are scarce, his significance lies in his lineage and his connection to the powerful storm god, Susanoo. He embodies the untamed aspects of nature and the sea, representing both its destructive and life-giving forces. His name itself suggests a relationship with the 'eight islands' (yashima) of Japan, possibly implying a protective role over the land from the ravages of the sea. As a descendant of Susanoo, he inherits a portion of the storm god's power and influence, making him a figure of respect and potential fear. He is a testament to the complex and interconnected nature of the Shinto pantheon, where deities are often defined by their relationships and associations with other gods and natural phenomena.

sources.title: Nihon Shoki
sources.author: Various authors, compiled by Prince Toneri
